findings.title: Deep relative import path in smoke test is fragile to directory restructuring
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `import('../../../../../lib/pdfWorker')` with `import('@/lib/pdfWorker')` — the Vite `@` alias (resolves to `./src`) is already configured in vite.config.ts:596 and works in dynamic imports during Vitest runs. The current 5-level `../` chain is hard to verify at a glance and would break if the PdfViewer test directory were moved or restructured.
findings.why_it_matters: Deep relative import chains of 5+ segments are fragile to directory restructuring and harder to verify correctly at a glance. The project already has a `@` alias configured in vite.config.ts that resolves to `./src`, making `@/lib/pdfWorker` the idiomatic, self-documenting alternative. Using the alias eliminates the need for the reader to mentally count directory levels and ensures the import survives future filesystem reorganization.
findings.evidence: The smoke test file at `src/app/components/figma/PdfViewer/__tests__/PdfViewer.smoke.test.tsx` imports `pdfWorker` via `await import('../../../../../lib/pdfWorker')`. Counting the path segments: `__tests__` → `PdfViewer` → `figma` → `components` → `app` → `src` (5 levels), resolving to `src/lib/pdfWorker.ts`. The `@` alias is defined at vite.config.ts line 596 as `'@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src')`, making `@/lib/pdfWorker` the equivalent path. The dynamic `import()` in Vitest respects Vite resolve aliases, so the substitution is drop-in compatible.
